meta.title: Donnie Darko — Illustrated Playthrough
meta.source: Calibrated against the live site (http://localhost:8080). Narrative cross-referenced with src/aid/aid1.html … aid9.html. See steps/donnie.guide.steps.json for the original 43-step guide.
meta.notes: Canvas-local coords (authored stage 0..800 x 0..500; rendered at 768x480, mapper scales). The intro montage and several screens animate perpetually (scanlines / a live-ticking counter), so those steps use mode:fixed — diff-stable can't settle on a never-static canvas. Static end-states use diff-stable.

steps.caption: The site opens on a Win98 pop-up cycling through distorted portraits, with a red bullseye target. Click the bullseye repeatedly to advance the intro montage.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-05-30. Bullseye is a fixed authored target at (478,250); each click advances one frame. ~3 clicks reach the proceed screen; the 4th is a harmless no-op. Montage scanlines animate constantly → mode:fixed.

steps.caption: The montage settles on “The Tangent Universe collapsed 8981 days … ago.” Click the red “.proceed.” link.
steps.note: CALIBRATED. The seconds counter ticks live, so this screen never diff-settles → mode:fixed. .proceed. link at ~(658,280).

steps.caption: A pop-up offers “if you have been here before, enter the Level 1 password” with a text field, or “otherwise proceed here”. As a first-time visitor, click “otherwise proceed here”. The screen then forwards automatically, assembling an animated text collage that resolves into a scanned detention-centre report on DARKO, DONALD J.
steps.note: CALIBRATED. The Level-1 password (breathe) cheat would go in the field at ~(310,205). The collage auto-forwards (~40s) to the static document (matches aid no2a.jpg). minWaitMs floors the wait so diff-stable can't latch onto the brief static gap before the collage builds.

steps.caption: Click the detention report to open the terminal affidavit: “AFFIDAVIT FOR WARRANT OF ARREST AND DETENTION … OFFENSE OF: ATTEMPTED ESCAPE - CLASS A”, ending with the prompt “C:>MORE ? (Y/N)”.
steps.note: CALIBRATED. Green terminal text (blinking cursor) → mode:fixed.

steps.caption: Type Y at the “C:>MORE ? (Y/N)” prompt to read the arresting officer's statement (Donnie found asleep in a field, sleepwalking), which ends with another “C:>MORE ? (Y/N)”.
steps.note: CALIBRATED. Keyboard reaches Ruffle via focused player. N does nothing in the original; Y advances.

steps.caption: Type Y again. The report dissolves into the Level 1 main menu — a cloudscape with a lone figure and several red crosshair hotspots labelled “something”, “close”, “I”, “want”…
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-05-30. Reaches the menu (matches aid no2c.jpg). Clouds drift subtly → looser threshold.

steps.caption: On the cloudscape menu, the drifting words are decoration; the three fixed red crosshairs are the level selectors. Hover the lower-centre crosshair (Level 1) and click — the screen tears apart into “THINGS AREN'T THAT SIMPLE!”, beginning Level 1.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-05-30, reproducible 2/2 (23% diff). clouds.swf button 0092 'level 1 is active' fires an internal go→_level2 (loads book.swf), so it works; levels 2 & 3 are gated until the prior level is completed. Crosshair must be clicked precisely (red-detected centre); hover initiates the rollover.

steps.caption: A thin red line leads up to the top-left. Click the first circle there — a Middlesex Times Dispatch obituary for Roberta Sparrow opens in a new browser tab. Note the surname (“a bird”), then close the tab to continue.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-05-30. Opens news/pop1.html in a NEW TAB; the runner screenshots then closes it (closing continues the game). Roberta Sparrow obituary → password 'her name was that of a bird' = sparrow.

steps.caption: A second circle further down the red line opens a second obituary (Dr Kenneth Monnitoff) in a new tab. Read it and close it; a third circle then appears.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-05-30. Opens news/pop2.html in a NEW TAB. After both obituaries a 3rd red crosshair appears at (490,151).

steps.caption: After both obituaries, a third red circle appears at upper-centre over the skeletal figure (“a guide in a time of great danger”). Click it: a banner unfurls reading “her name was that of a bird *******” — a seven-letter password prompt. The obituary named her Roberta Sparrow, so the answer is the bird: sparrow.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-05-31. Red-detected 3rd crosshair at canvas (490,151). Reveals the 'her name was that of a bird *******' hint (book.swf, readonly TextField9). The seven asterisks are a STATIC hint, not a live field — they never change. Banner text animates in → mode:fixed.

steps.caption: In the small password field below the hint, type the answer — sparrow — and press Enter. The banner reshapes into a letter: Karen P. Monnitoff (Library of Congress) forwarding “The Philosophy of Time Travel” by Roberta Sparrow to Elizabeth, with instructions to hide it where it can never be found.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-05-31. The pass field (book.swf EditText id 0120, password) sits inside the 'box' clip placed at root (211.95,209), field-local (1,4.25) → stage ~(213,213). Click-to-focus at (235,221) — earlier clicks at y≈155-185 hit the readonly hint ABOVE the field and never focused it. A per-frame loop checks `this.pass eq "sparrow"` and, on match, gotoAndStop(3)+_root.spear.gotoAndPlay → Monnitoff letter (matches aid l213.jpg). Typing does NOT clear the asterisk hint (separate field); Enter commits. Reaching this in a fresh full run leaves the field empty, so no pre-clear needed.

steps.caption: Click anywhere on the letter to continue. The page clears to white and a single red line of text fades in at lower-right: “DO YOU BELIEVE IN TIME TRAVEL ?”.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-05-31. Click on the letter body advances book.swf to the question screen (matches aid l214.jpg). The red question sits at lower-right, roughly canvas (590-775, 310).

steps.caption: Click the red “DO YOU BELIEVE IN TIME TRAVEL ?” question. The site navigates into the Tangent area: a dark room where the shadowy figure of Frank looms behind three Win98 pop-up windows spelling out a phrase — “remember”, “one”, “word”.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-05-31. Red-detected question text at canvas (646,311). This click NAVIGATES to a NEW page/SWF (the tangent area; a fresh Ruffle instance loads) — it is NOT a same-page transition. The three boxes read 'remember / one / word' (matches aid l215.jpg / step 13). NAVIGATION BOUNDARY: the next steps run on the new page in the live flow; do NOT use a `navigate` reload here — the tangent SWF is inert when loaded standalone. The hardware-accel overlay re-appears after the nav and must be re-suppressed before the next click.

steps.caption: Three Win98 pop-up windows are open, spelling the phrase “remember / one / word”. Close the first window (“remember”) using the X in its title bar. All three windows close together and a new pop-up appears, lower-right: the shadowy figure of Frank with a single word — “smurf”. Remember it.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-05-31 (probe-l1-xhair3 --tangent + owner's screen recording). smurf.swf has ONE Stop at the 3-window frame (39): clicking ANY window's X fires button 0013 (this._visible=0; this.gotoAndPlay(12); _root.play()), and frame 40 REMOVEs all three windows at once → builds the Frank window (JPEG id 0023) + 'smurf' text (id 0025) → Stop at frame 48. So a single close reveals Frank; there is no intermediate 'only window 3 left' state in this rebuild. X of the 'remember' window = title-bar close button at canvas (212,262) (rightmost of the _ □ X cluster; the window spans ~x30-225). Frank window animates in → mode:fixed.

steps.caption: Close the Frank pop-up (“smurf”) using the X in its title bar. The screen tears into a red fracture and the site navigates into the Tangent Universe — a destabilised mirror world (philosophy.swf), with the figure suspended at the top of the frame.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-05-31. The Frank window sits lower-right (~x562-787); its CLOSE X is at canvas (775,262) — ~13px left of the right border, the rightmost of the _ □ X cluster. NB earlier clicks at x≈745-758 hit the minimize/maximize buttons (no-op) — the X is further right at ~775. Closing it fires _root.play() → frames 49-54 → GetUrl 'universe/is_unstable.html'. NAVIGATION BOUNDARY: this loads a fresh SWF (philosophy.swf); the runner waits for the new Ruffle + re-suppresses the hw-accel overlay automatically. Per owner: on is_unstable.html the next Win98 window relocates to the TOP-RIGHT at the same 800×500 canvas. Red transition animates → mode:fixed (6s).

steps.caption: The Tangent Universe loads (philosophy.swf): the figure hangs at the top of the frame over a red fracture, and a tiny red crosshair sits at the far-left edge of the stage. Click it — the first Win98 pop-up window slides into the lower-left.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-01 (probe-grid-book). philosophy.swf autoplays a ~5.5s intro on load, so delayBeforeMs 9000 lets the far-left red diamond crosshair appear. Hit-area is the tiny diamond at the very edge — (45,330) MISSES; (12,330) hits. This click → _root.gotoAndStop/play('pop1') which click-steps the windows (NOT auto-played): each subsequent click summons the NEXT window (dad → donnie → straight → gran-donnie, one sub-swf loads per click). Scan-lined windows animate → mode:fixed.

steps.caption: Click the middle of the first window. A second Win98 window opens to the lower-right.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-01. Click the just-opened window's middle to advance to the next (loads donnie.swf). Windows rearrange leftward each step (owner: keep clicking each newest window's middle).

steps.caption: Click the middle of the second window. A third window opens.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-01. The right window's true middle is y≈350 (earlier y≈375-385 missed). Loads straight.swf.

steps.caption: Click the middle of the third window. The windows resolve into a 2×2 grid of four, with the grandmother/Donnie figure (gran-donnie) filling in behind them.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-01. Loads gran-donnie.swf — which ALSO reveals the page's transparent `[data-popup="gran-donnie.swf"]` HTML overlay (right:0, 60% width, z-index 999) that now covers the whole canvas. The grid keeps re-loading gran-donnie every frame (idle loop) until the hidden advance button is clicked next. Grid forms slowly → fixed 9000.

steps.caption: Click the bottom-right of the grid. The four windows take on “Microsoft Internet” title bars, then the scene dissolves and the Philosophy of Time Travel book builds itself from scattered words — finally a pop-up appears: the shadow of Frank asking “you found me… remember the word?”, with a password field.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-01. THE ADVANCE = hidden button id 0104 (`_root.gotoAndStop('words')`), placed on the main timeline (frame 108) only after the 4th window; its hit-rect is canvas x[258-435] y[290-467], centre (346,378). REQUIRES `neutralizeOverlays` — the gran-donnie span (above) sits at the SAME viewport region as the canvas (x512-1280) and eats the click otherwise. The book opens with a ~12s intro (words → book → Frank slides in) → fixed 14000 lands on Frank's window.

steps.caption: Type the word from Frank — smurf — into the field. It commits live: the screen fills with four Win98 windows each showing a Smurf, and red chapter crosshairs scatter down the left edge.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-01. Field = EditText id 0165 (`pass`, password); a per-frame check `if (pass eq 'smurf') gotoAndPlay('smurf')` commits — NO Enter. `locateField` finds Frank's navy titlebar at runtime and types into its centre + ~186px (the window repositions between runs, so don't hard-code). Focus is automatic (clicking focuses <ruffle-player>); the ONLY trick is hitting the editable rect (centre, not the bottom edge). Smurf windows carry grey '@.' titlebars (NOT navy) so they don't confuse titlebar detection.

steps.caption: Donnie’s letter to Roberta Sparrow lies underneath the Smurf grid — clicking a Smurf window flashes the grid hidden for under half a second, exposing the text. One word is red: “breathe” (“…I can breathe a sigh of relief…”). Click it to reveal the prize: “the word you just saw is your password to level 2.” Level 1 is complete; breathe is the Level 2 password.
steps.note: CALIBRATED 2026-06-01. The letter (red word `breathe` ≈ canvas (433,406)) sits UNDER the Smurf grid; clicking any Smurf window (button 0176) flashes the grid hidden <0.5s. Flash with the TOP-RIGHT window (620,120), NOT the bottom-left — breathe overlaps the bottom-left window, so flashing it would just re-hit it. Fixed burst of 8 taps @~0.11s (owner's tip) to beat the sub-second flash; breathe usually registers in 1-2 taps and the rest hit empty space harmlessly (verified: 6 extra taps cause no harm — flash(620,120) is empty once the grid clears and target(433,406) is over the book, not a button). NB do NOT gate on `until:navytitlebar` here — the Smurf grid windows ALSO carry navy 'Microsoft Internet' titlebars, so that stop condition false-fires. The advance is an INTERNAL SWF transition (URL unchanged) → no awaitNav. NEXT FRONTIER: the `level2.swf` HTML overlay (`<a href="/menu.html">`, revealed ~12s after the book) is the Level 2 gateway; Level 2's own puzzles are not yet calibrated. Closing the Roberta window instead lets you browse the book chapters (Level 3 clue = `rose`).
